summary: Iteration 169 - All requested features verified: Mill Entries row expand/collapse, PPR row click navigation to Mill Entries, Python round_amount function, Excel export date format DD-MM-YYYY, and login Enter key flow.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All features working correctly. Mill Entries table has expand/collapse on row click showing KMS Year, Season, Cutting, KG, Created By, Remark. PPR row click navigates to Mill Entries tab and highlights the entry. Python round_amount function correctly rounds >0.50 up and <=0.50 down. Excel export dates are in DD-MM-YYYY format.

mill_entries_row_expand_collapse.chevron_icon_visible: PASS - ChevronRight/ChevronDown icons visible on each row
mill_entries_row_expand_collapse.row_click_expands: PASS - Clicking row expands to show details
mill_entries_row_expand_collapse.expanded_details_visible: PASS - Shows KMS Year, Season, Cutting, KG, Created By, Created timestamp, Remark
mill_entries_row_expand_collapse.row_click_collapses: PASS - Clicking expanded row collapses it

ppr_row_click_navigation.ppr_table_visible: PASS - Paddy Purchase Register table with 6 entries
ppr_row_click_navigation.row_click_navigates: PASS - Clicking PPR row navigates to Mill Entries tab
ppr_row_click_navigation.entry_highlighted: PASS - Entry is highlighted with amber ring after navigation

login_enter_key_flow.username_enter: PASS - Enter key moves focus to password field
login_enter_key_flow.password_enter: PASS - Enter key submits login form

desktop_js_roundAmount_note: Desktop JS roundAmount function at /app/desktop-app/routes/safe_handler.js line 51 uses same logic: >0.50 rounds up (Math.ceil), <=0.50 rounds down (Math.trunc). Cannot test directly in web environment but code review confirms correct implementation.
